evidence_refs.quote: We investigate whether a verifier router that uses workflow metadata—workflow type, risk level, changed files, and acceptance tags—can select a smaller verifier set while preserving most of the defect-detection coverage of running every verifier. We implement a deterministic synthetic benchmark generating 420 workflow cases across seven workflow types with eight injected defect classes, and compare five routing strategies: run-all, workflow-aware, keyword-title, minimal-default, and random-budge

claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: However, the workflow-aware router showed systematic miss clusters in integration-break, UI accessibility, dependency-risk, and configuration-break defect families.
